metadata.notes: Wingspan standard overhaul v2: converted all prose paragraphs to structured bullets/lists across all tabs.

subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Cards (232 Total)
- 42 green vine cards — grape varieties showing red/white grape types, values 1-4, and Trellis/Irrigation requirements
- 36 purple wine order cards — score VP by delivering specific wines from your cellar
- 38 yellow summer visitor cards — one-time abilities usable during summer
- 38 blue winter visitor cards — one-time abilities usable during winter
- 18 field cards (3 per player, values 5, 6, and 7) — your grape-growing land
- 24 Automa cards for solo play
- 36 mama and papa cards — used during setup to give each player a unique starting position

#### Workers & Markers
- 5 regular workers per player — small meeples in your chosen color
- 1 grande worker per player — larger meeple that can be placed on any action space even if completely full
- 1 gray temporary worker — goes to whoever picks wake-up row 7 each round
- 1 wake-up token per player (rooster shape)
- 1 VP token per player (cork shape)
- 1 residual payment token per player (bottle shape)

#### Tokens, Coins & Structures
- 50 glass-shaped grape and wine tokens — for tracking values on crush pad and cellar
- 48 wooden structure tokens (8 types per player color)
- 72 lira coins in denominations of 1, 2, and 5
- 1 first-player grape token

#### Boards
- 1 main game board — seasonal action spaces, wake-up chart, and VP track around the edge
- 1 personal vineyard mat per player — where you plant vines, crush grapes, and age wines

#### Action Space Scaling
- **2 players** — 1 space per action
- **3-4 players** — 2 spaces per action
- **5-6 players** — 3 spaces per action

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Getting Started
1. **Choose first player** — randomly select
2. **Deal starting cards** — each player gets 1 mama card and 1 papa card, face up
3. **Resolve mamas first** — every mama gives you 2 regular workers plus a combination of card draws (vine cards, visitor cards, or wine order cards). Draw the cards shown and add to your hand.
4. **Resolve papas** — each papa gives you 1 grande worker plus lira, and often a choice between a free structure or extra lira. Resolve clockwise from first player.
5. **Take field cards** — each player takes their 3 field cards (values 5, 6, 7) and places them field-side up on their vineyard mat
6. **Discard mama and papa cards** — not needed after setup

#### Optional Advanced Setup
- **For experienced players** — deal 2 mamas and 2 papas to each player, choose 1 of each
- Adds more strategy to starting position but takes longer

#### After Setup You Should Have
- 2-3 regular workers + 1 grande worker
- Some lira coins
- A hand of cards from your mama
- Vineyard mat with 3 empty fields ready for planting

subtopics.title: Year Structure
subtopics.content:
#### The 5 Phases of Each Year

1. **Spring — Choose Your Wake-Up Time** — place your rooster token on the wake-up chart (rows 1-7). Lower numbers = earlier turn order. Higher numbers = better bonuses but later actions.
2. **Summer — Place Workers on Summer Actions** — in wake-up order, place 1 worker at a time on summer action spaces. Resolve each action immediately. Once you pass, you are locked out of summer for the rest of the year.
3. **Fall — Draw a Visitor Card** — each player draws either 1 summer visitor (yellow) or 1 winter visitor (blue).
4. **Winter — Place Workers on Winter Actions** — same rules as summer but using winter action spaces. 1 worker at a time, wake-up order, pass when done.
5. **Year End — Reset and Age** — all grapes and wines automatically age +1 value (max 9). Return all workers to supply. Collect residual payments. Rotate first player.

#### Wake-Up Bonuses
- **Row 1** — no bonus, but you act first
- **Row 2** — draw 1 vine card
- **Row 3** — draw 1 wine order card
- **Row 4** — gain 1 lira or 1 VP
- **Row 5** — draw 1 summer or winter visitor
- **Row 6** — gain 1 VP
- **Row 7** — gain the gray temporary worker for this year

#### How the Game Ends
- **Trigger** — any player reaches 20 or more VP
- **Resolution** — finish the current year (all remaining seasons play out)
- **Multiple winners possible** — several players can pass 20 VP in the same year

subtopics.title: Actions & Worker Placement
subtopics.content:
#### Core Worker Rules
- **1 worker per turn** — place exactly 1 worker on an available action space
- **Single-use per year** — a worker placed in summer cannot be used again in winter
- **Passing locks you out** — once you pass in a season, no more placements that season
- **Grande worker** — ignores ALL placement restrictions. Can be placed on any action space even if every slot is full, even if you already have a regular worker there. Lets you take the same action twice in one season.

#### Summer Actions
- **Draw vine card** — take 1 vine card from the deck. Bonus: +1 extra card.
- **Plant vine** — play a vine card from hand onto one of your three fields. Check Trellis/Irrigation requirements first. Bonus: +1 additional vine.
- **Give tour** — earn 2 lira. Bonus: +1 extra lira.
- **Build structure** — pay the lira cost and build on your vineyard mat. Bonus: +1 lira discount.
- **Play summer visitor** — play a yellow visitor card for its one-time effect. Bonus: +1 additional visitor.
- **Sell grapes / buy-sell field** — sell grapes for quick lira, or buy/sell fields. Bonus: +1 VP. You can only sell a field with no vines on it. Uprooting is free — just remove the vine card at any time on your turn.
- **Gain 1 lira (cart)** — unlimited spaces, always available.

#### Winter Actions
- **Draw wine order** — take 1 wine order card. Bonus: +1 extra card.
- **Harvest field** — choose 1 planted field and move grapes to your crush pad. Bonus: +1 additional field.
- **Make wine** — combine grapes from crush pad into wines in your cellar, making up to 2 wines. Bonus: +1 additional wine.
- **Fill wine order** — discard wines matching a wine order card to earn its VP reward. Bonus: +1 additional order.
- **Train worker** — pay 4 lira to gain 1 new worker. Bonus: cost reduced to 3 lira.
- **Play winter visitor** — play a blue visitor card for its one-time effect. Bonus: +1 additional visitor.
- **Gain 1 lira (cart)** — unlimited spaces, always available.

subtopics.title: Vine, Grape & Wine Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Planting Vines
- **Vine values** — each vine card shows red and/or white grape types with values 1-4
- **Field limits** — total combined value of all vines on a single field cannot exceed that field's value (5, 6, or 7)
- **Structure requirements** — some vines require Trellis, some Irrigation, some both. Check before planting.
- **Stacking** — multiple vines can share a field as long as the total stays within the field's value

#### Harvesting
- **Combine by color** — add up ALL red grape values on a field's vine cards into a single red grape token on your crush pad. Do the same for white grapes.
- **Example** — a field with red 2 vine + red 3 vine = one red grape token at value 5
- **Max value** — grape and wine values cap at 9
- **Vines stay planted** — harvesting does not remove vines. Harvest the same field every year.

#### Making Wine
- **Red wine** — made from red grapes. Wine value = grape value used.
- **White wine** — made from white grapes. Wine value = grape value used.
- **Blush wine** — 1 red grape + 1 white grape. Wine value = sum of both. Requires Medium Cellar.
- **Sparkling wine** — 2 red grapes + 1 white grape. Wine value = sum of all three. Requires Large Cellar.

#### Aging
- **Automatic and free** — at year end, ALL grapes on crush pad and ALL wines in cellar gain +1 value (max 9)
- **No action required** — aging is mandatory and happens every year
- **Strategic lever** — a wine worth 3 this year becomes 4 next year without spending an action

#### Filling Orders
- **Match requirements** — wine order cards list specific wine types and minimum values
- **Meet or exceed** — your wines must meet or exceed the required values (a value-6 wine fills a value-4 requirement)
- **No combining** — you cannot combine two lower-value wines to meet a single requirement
- **Earn VP** — discard matching wines from your cellar to earn the order's VP reward

subtopics.title: Structures
subtopics.content:
#### The 8 Structures
- **Trellis (2 lira)** — required to plant certain higher-value vine cards. If your starting vines show a trellis requirement, build this first.
- **Irrigation (3 lira)** — required for certain vine plantings. Combined with Trellis, unlocks every vine in the deck.
- **Yoke (2 lira)** — harvest or uproot 1 additional field each time you take that action. Best with 2+ planted fields producing different grapes.
- **Windmill (5 lira)** — gain 1 VP each time you plant a vine. Only worth building if you plan to plant many vines.
- **Cottage (4 lira)** — draw 1 extra card during the Fall visitor draw phase. Compounds over time.
- **Tasting Room (6 lira)** — gain 1 VP each time you give a tour, as long as you have at least 1 wine in your cellar. Expensive but powerful with a dedicated tour strategy.
- **Medium Cellar (4 lira)** — store wines up to value 6 and enables blush wine (1 red + 1 white grape). Without this, wines cap at value 3.
- **Large Cellar (6 lira)** — store wines up to value 9 and enables sparkling wine (2 red + 1 white grapes). Includes all Medium Cellar benefits, so you don't need both.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### How the Game Ends
- **Trigger** — any player reaches 20+ VP at any point
- **Resolution** — finish the current year (all remaining seasons play out normally)
- **Multiple players** — several players can cross 20 VP in the same final year

#### Scoring
- **All VP tracked during the game** — no separate end-game scoring phase. Every VP earned goes immediately on the VP track.
- **Final score** — your position on the VP track at the end of the last year

#### VP Sources
- **Wine orders** — primary source, worth 2-6 VP each
- **Visitor card effects** — some award VP directly
- **Wake-up bonuses** — rows 4 and 6 give VP
- **Windmill** — 1 VP per vine planted
- **Tasting Room** — 1 VP per tour given (with wine in cellar)

#### Tiebreakers
1. **Most lira remaining**
2. **Highest-value wine** in your cellar
3. **Highest-value grape** on your crush pad

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Opening Priorities
- **Build the pipeline first.** Years 1-2, focus on planting vines and basic infrastructure. Draw vine cards, plant vines, build Trellis/Irrigation as needed. Without planted vines, you can't harvest, make wine, or fill orders.
- **Protect your turn order when it matters.** Take early wake-up (rows 1-3) when you need a contested action space. Take later rows (5-7) for bonuses only when timing doesn't matter.
- **Save your Grande Worker for the choke point.** Hold it for the ONE blocked action you must have — usually Fill Wine Order or Make Wine in winter.
- **Respect the field math.** Fields hold vines up to their printed values: 5, 6, and 7. A field worth 7 can hold a red 4 vine + white 3 vine for maximum output.
- **Don't overbuild.** Only build structures your pipeline needs now. A Large Cellar is useless without planted vines. Build Trellis first, then Irrigation if needed, then a cellar when you have grapes ready.

#### Common Mistakes
- Choosing a late wake-up row for the bonus while losing a critical action space to another player
- Burning all workers in summer with none left for winter — workers placed in summer can't be reused in winter
- Using your Grande Worker on a replaceable action early, then getting blocked on Fill Wine Order or Make Wine later
- Selling grapes for quick lira instead of making wine for VP — grapes are worth far more as wine
- Ignoring aging — grapes and wines gain +1 value for free every year-end. Plan around this.

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of Viticulture

**Setup Decision**: Resolve your mama card first (2 regular workers + starting cards), then your papa card (1 grande worker + lira, usually a choice between a free structure or extra lira). Check your vine cards — do any require Trellis or Irrigation? That tells you what to build first.

**Year 1 — Spring**
- Place your rooster on the wake-up chart
- Row 2 (draw a vine card) and Row 5 (draw a visitor card) are safe first-year choices
- Avoid Row 1 unless you desperately need to act first

**Year 1 — Summer**
*Priority order:*
1. If you have a vine you can plant (check Trellis/Irrigation requirements), plant it
2. If your best vine needs a Trellis, build one first
3. If you can't do either, draw vine cards to improve your options
- **Critical** — save 1-2 workers for winter! Spending everything in summer is a common first-game mistake.

**Year 1 — Fall**
- Draw a winter visitor card (blue) — winter visitors tend to have powerful economic effects for the early game (gaining resources, training workers at a discount, free structures)

**Year 1 — Winter**
*Ideal play with remaining workers:*
1. Harvest a planted field to move grapes to your crush pad
2. Make Wine to turn those grapes into bottles in your cellar
- If you can't harvest (no planted vines), Train a Worker (pay 4 lira) for more actions next year

**Year End**
- All grapes and wines automatically age +1 value
- Workers return to supply
- Collect any residual payments

**Years 2-3 — Building the Engine**
- Run the full pipeline each year: Summer (plant vines, build structures) into Winter (harvest, make wine, fill orders)
- The first time you fill a wine order for VP, the whole engine clicks into place

**The Grande Moment**
- When the action you need is blocked by other players, your Grande Worker saves the day
- Save it for critical moments — filling wine orders or making wine when every slot is taken

**First Game Target**
- Aim to fill 3-4 wine orders over the game
- A score of 18-22 VP is solid for a first game
- Focus on understanding the seasonal rhythm and how each year your vineyard gets more productive

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### The Three Paths to Victory
- **Path 1: Wine Order Engine** — plant high-value vines, harvest every year, make wine, fill orders. Most reliable strategy. Key structures: Trellis + Irrigation for best vines, then Large Cellar for sparkling wine.
- **Path 2: Visitor Card Engine** — lean on visitor cards for VP and resources. Some visitors award 2-4 VP directly. Key structure: Cottage for extra Fall draws every year. Higher variance but can score fast.
- **Path 3: Hybrid (Recommended)** — run a basic wine pipeline for consistent VP while using visitors to accelerate. Fill orders as baseline, let visitor spikes push you over 20.

#### Structure Priority
1. **Trellis (2 lira)** — unlocks the best vine cards. Almost always your first build.
2. **Irrigation (3 lira)** — combined with Trellis, unlocks every vine in the deck.
3. **Medium Cellar (4 lira)** — blush wine is the most efficient wine type. Red 3 + white 2 = blush 5, often enough for mid-range orders.
4. **Large Cellar (6 lira)** — sparkling wine for the highest-value orders. Build when you have grapes ready.
5. **Cottage (4 lira)** — extra visitor draws compound every year. Better the earlier you build it.
6. **Yoke (2 lira)** — double harvests with 2+ planted fields producing different grape types.
7. **Windmill (5 lira)** — 1 VP per vine planted. Only worth it if planting many vines.
8. **Tasting Room (6 lira)** — only with a dedicated tour strategy. Niche but powerful when committed.

#### Blush Wine: The Secret Weapon
- **Why it's efficient** — combines two lower-value grapes into one higher-value wine
- **Example** — red 3 grape + white 2 grape = blush wine worth 5, often enough to fill a 4 or 5-value requirement
- **Low barrier** — only requires Medium Cellar (4 lira), much cheaper than Large Cellar (6 lira) for sparkling

#### Wake-Up Mastery
- **Row 1** — take when you MUST go first for a contested action space. Cost: giving up a bonus.
- **Row 4** — always take the VP, not the lira. 1 VP is almost always worth more than 1 lira.
- **Row 6** — free VP with decent turn order. Great default when nothing else is urgent.
- **Row 7** — extra temporary worker = extra action this year. Take when turn order doesn't matter and you need volume.

#### Worker Management
- **Train 4th worker by Year 2** — more workers = more actions per year = faster pipeline
- **Don't exceed 5 regular workers** — returns diminish and the 4 lira cost adds up
- **Split between seasons** — 2-3 in summer, 2-3 in winter. Dumping all workers in one season is a common mistake.

#### Player Count Strategy
- **2 Players** — only 1 space per action, blocking is brutal. Grande Worker is critical. Every wake-up decision is high-stakes.
- **3-4 Players** — the sweet spot. 2 spaces per action means manageable competition with room to maneuver.
- **5-6 Players** — 3 spaces per action, rarely blocked. Visitor card strategies get stronger with longer games giving more card draws.

#### Key Decision Points
1. **Year 1** — build Trellis now or plant a basic vine? Usually build Trellis — it unlocks much better vines.
2. **Year 2-3** — fill a small order early or wait for aged wine? Early VP is usually better — get on the board.
3. **Mid-game** — upgrade cellar when you actually have grapes ready to use it, not before.
4. **Late game (15-18 VP)** — every action must point at VP. Play visitors for VP spikes, fill best remaining orders, don't waste actions on infrastructure.

rules_citations.source: Official Stonemaier Games Rulebook — Viticulture Essential Edition

key_rulings.text: The grande worker may be placed on an action even if that action is completely full (it does not need the bonus slot). This includes spaces where you already have your own regular worker — the grande worker ignores ALL occupancy limits, allowing you to take the same action twice in one season.
Once a worker is placed in summer, it cannot be used again in winter that same year. Workers placed in either season are returned to your supply at year end.

key_rulings.topic: Game end trigger — finish the year
key_rulings.text: If any player reaches 20 or more victory points, the current year is completed (all remaining seasons are played). Then the game ends. Multiple players may exceed 20 VP.
When you harvest a field, you move grape tokens to your crush pad based on the planted vine values. The vines remain on the field and may be harvested again in future years.
At the end of each year, all grape tokens on the crush pad and all wine tokens in the cellar increase in value by 1, to a maximum of 9. This happens automatically and cannot be skipped.
Red and white wines can be stored in the basic cellar (values 1-3). Blush wine (1 red + 1 white grape) requires the Medium Cellar. Sparkling wine (2 red + 1 white grapes) requires the Large Cellar.
Each field has a value of 5, 6, or 7. The total combined value of all vine cards planted on a single field cannot exceed that field's value.
A player's victory point total cannot go below negative 5 (-5 VP). You may not play a visitor card or take an action that would reduce your VP below -5.
Once you pass in a season (summer or winter), you may not place any more workers during that season. You are locked out for the remainder of that season.

You may only sell a field that does not have a vine on it. To sell a planted field, first uproot the vines — uprooting is a free action (just remove the vine card from the field at any time on your turn). It does NOT require a worker or the Harvest action.

key_rulings.topic: Wine order filling — wines must meet or exceed
key_rulings.text: When filling a wine order, the wines you discard from your cellar must meet or exceed the values specified on the order card. You may use wines of higher value than required. You cannot substitute wine types or combine multiple lower-value wines to meet a single requirement.

key_rulings.topic: Grape values combine per color when harvesting
key_rulings.text: When you harvest a field, you add up ALL the red grape values on vine cards in that field into a single red grape token, and all the white grape values into a single white grape token. You do NOT get one grape token per vine card.

key_rulings.topic: Wine or grape devaluation when slot is occupied
key_rulings.text: If you would create a wine or grape token at a value where the slot is already occupied, the token devalues to the next lowest available slot. If no slot is available, the token is lost.

key_rulings.topic: Bonus action ordering — bonus before or after
key_rulings.text: When you place a worker on an action space that has a bonus, you may take the action and the bonus in any order you choose. For example, you can collect the 1 lira bonus from the Build bonus space first and use it toward the structure cost.

key_rulings.topic: Visitor cards — must be able to resolve mandatory effects
key_rulings.text: You may only play a visitor card if you are able to fully resolve all mandatory effects on the card. If a card says 'do X to gain Y,' you must be able to do X. If a card offers choices with 'OR,' you only need to fulfill one option.
